title: Word measures on unitary groups
speaker: Doron Puder
abstract: *Please excuse the first 45seconds of video, due to technical issues, the video breaks up* This is joint work with Michael Magee. We combine concepts from random matrix theory and free probability together with ideas from the theory of commutator length in groups and maps from surfaces, and establish new connections between the two. More particularly, we study measures induced by free words on the unitary groups $U(n)$.

proof_stages.label: Word maps, word measures, and the orbit problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines word maps and word measures, recalls motivating results on word maps, and poses the guiding question whether equality of all word measures is exactly equality up to the automorphism group of the free group.

proof_stages.label: Trace statistic for U(n) word measures
proof_stages.summary: The speaker specializes to word measures on unitary groups and chooses the unnormalized expected trace as the first statistic, recalling Voiculescu’s o(n) asymptotic-freeness result and the Collins--Śniady/Weingarten rationality and computability of this statistic.

proof_stages.label: Vanishing outside the commutator subgroup
proof_stages.summary: Using Haar invariance under multiplication by central scalar matrices, the expected trace is shown to vanish identically whenever some generator has nonzero total exponent, reducing the problem to words in the commutator subgroup.

proof_stages.label: Commutator length controls the first possible order
proof_stages.summary: From explicit rational trace computations, the first main theorem identifies commutator length g as giving the upper bound O(n^{1-2g}) for the expected trace, with Culler’s surface interpretation explaining the invariant.

proof_stages.label: Leading coefficients as commutator-equation solution counts
proof_stages.summary: The leading coefficient is first modeled by counting solutions of the equation expressing w as a product of g commutators, modulo the natural Aut_delta(F_{2g}) action preserving the boundary commutator.

proof_stages.label: Euler-characteristic correction by stabilizers
proof_stages.summary: Because the Aut_delta action can fix a solution, the correct leading coefficient is a sum over solution orbits of the Euler characteristics of their stabilizer groups, whose finite K(G,1) models are established.

proof_stages.label: Stable commutator length as an audible invariant
proof_stages.summary: A corollary of the more general moment results, together with Calegari’s rationality theorem, is that stable commutator length can be recovered from the U(n) word measures even though ordinary commutator length cannot always be read directly.

proof_stages.label: Surface and poset model for the proof
proof_stages.summary: The proof is sketched geometrically by replacing commutator-equation solutions with Culler surface maps to a bouquet, identifying Aut_delta with a mapping class group, and constructing a contractible poset of arc-swap refinements whose quotients model the stabilizers.
